title: The Works of William Herebert, OFM
summary: The General Corpus Herebert XML is a repository copy of a March 1996 University of Michigan structured encoding derived from Oxford Text Archive deposit 1213. The OTA payload explicitly reproduces Stephen R. Reimer's 1987 PIMS edition of six Latin sermons, three Latin sermon outlines, and twenty-three Middle English poems from British Library Add MS 46919. The cataloged unit is this bounded author-focused selection, not the whole manuscript and not all matter in the printed edition. Reimer's appendix seventh sermon by another hand, printed front matter, notes, and apparatus are excluded from the digital text. The one evidenced physical witness is Add MS 46919; all twenty-three DIMEV poem records point to that codex, with one additional trial-draft occurrence for Poem 13. No complete public codex facsimile was found; one cropped f. 205r image is cached, and the manifest records current British Library consultation and whole-object digitisation routes.
review_notes: The thirty-two selected intellectual items are six sermons, three sermon outlines, and twenty-three poems; group containers are not counted as additional works.
The exact manuscript portion is discontiguous: f. 85r; ff. 159r–162v; f. 164v; ff. 165r–167v; ff. 169r–179v; ff. 181v–184v; f. 204v; and ff. 205r–211v.
The U-M file reorders the OTA payload from poems-sermons-outlines to sermons-outlines-poems and converts legacy markup; it is a version, not a byte-identical copy.
Rights are stated separately for medieval text, edition, deposit, encoding, repository artifact, databases, scholarship, and images.

entities.label: General Corpus Herebert XML
entities.description: The sparse repository artifact at CME/source/CME_from_OTA/Herebert.xml, pinned through the CME submodule.
notes: The containing General Corpus baseline for this research branch is f402373e75529e9db252c530051f153f2f8baf55.
The current file contains 520 L elements: 447 in poem divisions and 73 embedded in sermons.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse: Herebert
alternate_labels: The Works of William Herebert, OFM / William Herebert [electronic edition]
entities.description: A March 1996 structured electronic edition representing the same thirty-two selected items as OTA 1213, reordered and converted for navigation.
notes: The file says front matter is omitted, adds folio references for navigation, and omits printed editorial notes.
Small character-level interventions are observable, but their responsible agent and conversion specification are not documented in the checked sources.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1213: Works of William Herebert
entities.description: Stephen R. Reimer's 1988 OTA deposit, consisting of a documentation file and a legacy-markup text file that reproduces the Reimer edition's selected sermons, outlines, and poems.
notes: The current and legacy sequence-3 headers are byte-identical at 6,150 bytes and SHA-256 4a0c706663aff285837922eb1f15f7ed7d9c9fbabc1ecb8101f28d803a26c1f5.
The text order is twenty-three poems, six sermons, then three outlines.

entities.label: Stephen R. Reimer, ed., The Works of William Herebert, OFM (1987)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of OTA 1213: Reimer's author-focused edition from Add MS 46919, including introduction and apparatus absent from the digital payload and an appendix sermon by another hand absent from the thirty-two-item selection.

bibliographic.citation: Stephen R. Reimer, ed., The Works of William Herebert, OFM, Studies and Texts 81 (Toronto: Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies, 1987), x + 173 pp. and plate.
bibliographic.title: The Works of William Herebert, OFM

notes: Pritchard describes light emendation of obvious error, supplied missing words and details, modern punctuation, and selective incorporation of marginal or interlinear material.
The digital selection omits the printed introduction, notes, bibliography, indexes, plate, and appendix seventh sermon.

entities.label: London, British Library, Add MS 46919
entities.description: The sole physical codex evidenced for all thirty-two selected items: a multi-scribe English parchment miscellany containing Herebert's sermons, outlines, and poems among other material.

date_statements.label: Production
date_statements.value: England, before 1333?; catalog record date range 1332–1334
date_statements.notes: Individual portions and hands may have different dates; Sermon 3 is explicitly dated 9 June 1314.

holding.notes: Current catalog title: William Herebert Collection.

notes: Written by multiple hands; scholarship identifies approximately thirteen hands.
The selected work portion is discontiguous and does not include the entire codex.

BL item 48 covers sermons and notes on ff. 159–179 but excepts the other-hand appendix sermon on ff. 163–164v; item 50 covers ff. 181v–184v; item 54 covers f. 204v; item 55 covers nineteen lyrics on ff. 205–211v.
Poems 20–23 occur separately on ff. 161r, 179v, 204v, and 85r.
The main nineteen-poem sequence on ff. 205r–211v is associated with Herefordshire language; the codex as a whole should not be assigned that single localization.

entities.label: Cropped image of British Library Add MS 46919, f. 205r
entities.description: A 602 × 245-pixel cropped strip identified by Stephen R. Reimer's University of Alberta manuscript-course index as Add MS 46919, f. 205r. It is not a full folio or complete-codex surrogate.
notes: The exact vertical extent and current British Library image identifier are unresolved.

entities.label: Internet Archive restricted scan of Reimer 1987
entities.description: A complete-volume scan entry whose book files are access-restricted; public metadata and scandata establish pagination and preview structure.
entities.reproduction_format: restricted page-image scan with public metadata and scandata

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records for the twenty-three Herebert poems
entities.description: The twenty-three DIMEV records corresponding to Reimer's poem items. They identify twenty-four occurrences in Add MS 46919 because record 4895 includes both a seven-line f. 84v trial draft and the selected final version at f. 208v.
notes: Record 4895's final f. 208v version is encoded; the f. 84v trial draft is collateral context and is outside the selected text.
All twenty-four occurrences are in one physical codex, not twenty-four independent witnesses.

entities.label: Manuscripts of the West Midlands record 46 for Add MS 46919
entities.description: A scholarly manuscript description supplying collation, hand coverage, and dialect context for the Herebert codex.

entities.label: Telfryn Pritchard's 1988 review of Reimer's edition
entities.description: A three-page scholarly review documenting the edition's manuscript basis, selection, editorial practice, contents, appendix boundary, dialect characterization, and paratext.

bibliographic.citation: Telfryn Pritchard, review of The Works of William Herebert, OFM, Scriptorium 42, no. 2 (1988): 264–266.

entities.label: Christopher Fletcher, “Archiving Practices and the ‘Autograph’ Literary Manuscript” (2024)
entities.description: Current scholarship on autograph literary manuscripts that discusses Herebert material in Add MS 46919 and its light revision while preserving the codex's multi-author and multi-scribe complexity.

bibliographic.citation: Christopher Fletcher, “Archiving Practices and the ‘Autograph’ Literary Manuscript,” 2024, doi:10.1075/chlel.xxxv.29fle.

agents.name: Anonymous scribes of British Library Add MS 46919
notes: A collective contextual agent; the manuscript is not wholly in Herebert's hand.

agents.name: Nicholas Bozon
notes: Source responsibility for selected English adaptations does not itself establish authorship of every associated English item.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse
Digital Humanities Institute, University of Sheffield

object.description: The U-M Herebert structured encoding as represented by the pinned XML.

scope.notes: The repository artifact preserves the U-M header, editorial declaration, body order, and structure.

subject.description: All six sermons, three outlines, and twenty-three poems in U-M order.

object.description: All six sermons, three outlines, and twenty-three poems in OTA order.

scope.notes: The intellectual-item boundary is identical, but U-M reorders the groups, converts legacy markup, promotes folio and occasion comments, moves authorial notes, and makes small character interventions.

subject.description: The complete two-file OTA textual deposit.

object.description: The six sermons, three sermon outlines, and twenty-three poems, excluding printed introduction, apparatus, bibliography, indexes, plate, and appendix sermon.

scope.notes: The OTA coding guide explicitly says the files reproduce Reimer's text and PIMS copyright applies.

subject.description: The thirty-two-item digital selection in the main edition.

scope.notes: Reimer also prints a seventh, other-hand sermon from ff. 163r–164v in an appendix; that appendix is outside this relation's subject scope.

object.description: A cropped strip of f. 205r, not the whole leaf.

scope.notes: The parent course index identifies the folio; the crop itself does not expose a repository canvas identifier.
Only public metadata, scandata, and a limited viewer preview were retained; no restricted book payload was cached.

subject.description: Twenty-three DIMEV records and twenty-four recorded occurrences.

object.description: The twenty-three selected poem items plus the collateral seven-line trial draft of Poem 13.

scope.notes: The records describe one physical codex; occurrence count must not be flattened into a witness count.

subject.description: The MWM codicological and linguistic manuscript description.

object.description: British Library Add MS 46919 as a physical codex.
Reimer's edition, including its appendix and paratext.

subject.description: The chapter's discussion of Herebert and autograph literary manuscripts.

object.description: Herebert-associated writing and light revision in the codex, not every item or hand.

scope.notes: This relation provides current contextual scholarship; it does not assign blanket Herebert authorship to the codex.

access.access_method: Open the exact British Library Archives and Manuscripts record.
Submit the British Library collection-item request form, provide the shelfmark Add MS 46919, and supply a letter of introduction if requested.
access.format: Onsite consultation subject to access approval

access.access_method: No complete public page-image object was identified in the responsible catalog or corroborating manuscript portals.
notes: The BL record's digitised field was blank.
ARLIMA explicitly stated 'Ms. non numérisé' and showed a 2 July 2026 update date.
Biblissima's Mirador interface reported no digitized object; generic IIIF interface boilerplate was not treated as a canvas inventory.
The separately cached f. 205r crop is partial and does not change this complete-facsimile finding.

access.access_method: Request complete current-object photography from BL Digitisation Services, naming Add MS 46919 and asking for boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, edges, and detached matter where offered; separately identify the exact selected folios and ask BL to enumerate omissions.

local_copies.label: British Library Imaging Services rate card 2025–26
notes: The two-page rate-card PDF is completely downloaded; metadata_only describes its coverage relative to manuscript-facsimile access.

access.format: Quote-dependent photographic TIFFs; request colour explicitly
notes: The checked rate card listed £27.70 for the first photographic TIFF and £7.25 for each subsequent TIFF.
Original manuscripts require photography; fulfillment and complete-surface coverage are not guaranteed.
No order was placed.

rights.component: Medieval textual content represented by the selected passages
rights.basis: The selected sermons, outlines, and poems were created in the early fourteenth century; no modern rights statement is extended to their underlying medieval textual content.
notes: This does not determine rights in modern editions, metadata, photography, or access-delivered reproductions.

rights.component: Editorial text, introduction, apparatus, notes, typography, and plate in Reimer's 1987 edition
rights.basis: The edition's copyright page states © 1987 Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies, and the OTA coding guide repeats that copyright.
notes: No open license for the printed edition was found.

rights.component: OTA deposit files and encoded electronic edition
rights.jurisdiction: Provider statement; reuse jurisdiction not independently determined
rights.basis: The current OTA record applies CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 to the deposit while the embedded coding guide preserves PIMS's © 1987 restriction for the reproduced edition.
notes: The provider license is not treated as proof that OTA could relicense all upstream PIMS editorial matter.
Seek clarification for reuse beyond clearly provider-created encoding layers.

rights.component: U-M digital presentation and structured encoding
rights.basis: The current U-M item displays educational/research use language and a DPLA No Copyright – United States statement; upstream PIMS and OTA notices remain separately recorded.
notes: This provider assertion does not erase the source edition's explicit copyright notice.

rights.component: Repository XML artifact, including copied text and repository serialization
rights.basis: The pinned CME LICENSE uses the Unlicense for software, but it does not clearly resolve copied PIMS editorial text, OTA encoding rights, or manuscript-image rights.
notes: No blanket public-domain or open-license claim is made for every component of the XML.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and controlled-access delivery
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: The item is access-restricted/print-disabled and exposes no open reuse license for the complete scan.

rights.jurisdiction: Source and reuse jurisdictions not independently determined
rights.basis: The archived source course site asserted all rights reserved and no image-specific open license or British Library reuse grant was found.
notes: Wayback availability is access evidence, not a reuse license.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 database content and release archive
rights.basis: The Zenodo release declares Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.

rights.component: MWM scholarly manuscript description and website presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider and reuse jurisdictions not independently determined
rights.basis: Public access was verified, but no record-specific open reuse license was established.

rights.component: Pritchard's review text and Persée page images
rights.basis: Persée metadata marks the item free to access but does not supply an open reuse license for the review text or rendered pages.
The ORA record and PDF state Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Herebert-associated material dispersed through a multi-scribe codex
editorial_practices.representation: Six sermons, three outlines, and twenty-three poems in the main author-focused edition; a seventh other-hand sermon printed separately in an appendix
editorial_practices.description: Reimer organizes an author-focused selection from Add MS 46919 rather than reproducing the entire codex.
editorial_practices.scope: Main edited selection and excluded appendix boundary

editorial_practices.representation: Modern punctuation in the edited text
editorial_practices.description: Pritchard reports that Reimer supplied modern punctuation while otherwise limiting emendation to obvious error and supplied omissions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Marginal and interlinear additions, revisions, and alternate material
editorial_practices.representation: Material directed for insertion is incorporated; other revisions remain notes or variants
editorial_practices.description: The edition distinguishes inserted authorial material from alternate or revised matter rather than silently flattening all margins into the base text.

editorial_practices.representation: Two digital payload files containing only the six sermons, three outlines, and twenty-three poems
editorial_practices.description: The OTA deposit reproduces the selected texts but not the complete printed volume's scholarly front matter and apparatus.

editorial_practices.source_feature: OTA legacy comments, ordering, and Reimer print paratext
editorial_practices.representation: Sermons first, then outlines and poems; folio references and occasion comments promoted for navigation; print notes and front matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M transformation preserves the thirty-two-item boundary but changes order and structure for navigation.

evidence.locator: HEADER, editorial declaration, and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Fixes the current repository artifact, U-M title and date, Reimer source statement, omitted print matter, folio navigation, body order, item boundary, and exact encoded content.

evidence.summary: Uses Unlicense wording for software; does not expressly resolve incorporated PIMS editorial text or upstream digital-text layers.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, Herebert item.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current public U-M item identity and access route.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Herebert item rights display and DPLA No Copyright – United States statement.
evidence.summary: Provides current layer-specific U-M access and rights evidence; it is retained alongside, not substituted for, the embedded PIMS and OTA notices.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1213 current record and 6,150-byte sequence-3 header.
evidence.locator: deposit metadata, file map, revision history, source, languages, creation dates, and license
evidence.summary: Identifies Reimer as depositor, University of Alberta affiliation, deposit date 1 February 1988, OTA number 1213, PIMS source edition, languages enm/lat, creation range 1300–1333, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 provider license.
notes: Current and legacy sequence-3 responses were independently verified byte-for-byte identical.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive 1213, herebdoc-1213.txt.
evidence.summary: States that the files reproduce Reimer's text, identifies the proprietary Chicago-guide-inspired tag system, and repeats © 1987 PIMS and a permission instruction.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive 1213, herebtxt-1213.txt.
evidence.summary: Fixes the legacy-markup poem-sermon-outline order, folio comments, authorial-note handling, and all thirty-two selected items.

evidence.citation: Direct item-normalized comparison of OTA herebtxt-1213.txt with pinned U-M Herebert.xml.
evidence.summary: Confirms the identical bounded item set and documents reordering, markup conversion, promoted headings/milestones, moved authorial notes, and small character interventions; the two files are not byte copies.

evidence.citation: Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies, The Works of William Herebert, OFM, Studies and Texts 81.
evidence.summary: Confirms editor, title, January 1987 publication, series 81, extent x + 173 and plate, current ISBN, and current purchase availability.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive controlled-access viewer inspection of Reimer 1987, pages n5–n8.
evidence.locator: n5 plate, n6 title page, n7 copyright/CIP page, n8 dedication
evidence.summary: Confirms the printed title matter, © 1987 PIMS notice, old ISBN, Latin and Middle English language statement, and plate placement.
notes: No obfuscated viewer payload was retained.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scandata for the restricted Reimer 1987 scan.
evidence.summary: Confirms identifier, restriction state, pagination structure, languages, English introduction, references on pp. 149–162, and indexes.

evidence.citation: Telfryn Pritchard, review of Stephen R. Reimer, The Works of William Herebert, OFM, Scriptorium 42.2 (1988), 264–266.
evidence.summary: Corroborates the one-manuscript basis, multi-hand codex, six sermons, three outlines, twenty-three lyrics, appendix other-hand sermon, South-West Midlands dialect, textual sources, authorial revision, editorial intervention policy, plate, bibliography, and indexes.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 46919, William Herebert Collection, record 032-002103037.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, historical Phillipps designation, parchment extent and dimensions, approximate date, multi-hand character, exact sermon and poem item loci, the other-hand sermon exception, and current blank digitised field.

evidence.citation: British Library collection-item request and reference enquiry forms.
evidence.summary: Provides the current route for requesting physical consultation and reference assistance.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, request form, and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.summary: Provides the current request route and terms for quote-dependent photography of original manuscripts.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services rate card 2025–26.
evidence.summary: Lists £27.70 for the first photographic TIFF and £7.25 for each subsequent TIFF and distinguishes photography for original materials.

evidence.citation: Corroborated complete-facsimile audit of the BL record, ARLIMA Add MS 46919 entry, and Biblissima selection viewer.
evidence.locator: BL digitised field; ARLIMA digitization statement and update date; Biblissima viewer object state
evidence.summary: No complete public codex surrogate was found: BL exposed no digitized object, ARLIMA said the manuscript was not digitized and showed a 2 July 2026 update, and Biblissima reported no digitized object in the selection.
notes: This is a dated negative-access finding, not proof that no private, licensed, or future surrogate exists.

evidence.citation: Archived Stephen R. Reimer University of Alberta manuscript-course image and parent index, identifying hbt-lg.jpg as Add MS 46919, f. 205r.
evidence.summary: Provides one partial manuscript image and its folio identification; it does not cover the whole leaf or codex.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse v1.0, Zenodo record 14585961.
evidence.summary: Provides poem identities, Add MS 46919 loci, Reimer page references, source and attribution notes, and the two-occurrence trial-draft/final-version distinction for record 4895.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts of the West Midlands, manuscript record 46, British Library Add MS 46919.
evidence.summary: Provides collation, hand coverage, Herebert associations, and Herefordshire linguistic context for the main poem sequence.

evidence.citation: Christopher Fletcher, “Archiving Practices and the ‘Autograph’ Literary Manuscript” (2024), doi:10.1075/chlel.xxxv.29fle.
evidence.locator: discussion of Herebert and Add MS 46919
evidence.summary: Supplies current context for autograph literary manuscripts and light revision without collapsing the multi-scribe codex into a blanket Herebert attribution.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item record for the print-disabled Reimer 1987 volume.
evidence.summary: Confirms controlled/restricted access and the absence of a directly downloadable lawful complete public book payload.

evidence.citation: Persée record metadata for Pritchard's review.
evidence.summary: Marks the review free to access but supplies no record-specific open reuse license.

open_questions.question: How should the OTA CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 provider license be reconciled with the embedded © 1987 PIMS permission statement and U-M No Copyright – United States display?
next_steps: Ask OTA, U-M, and PIMS to identify which components each statement covers.
Obtain explicit PIMS permission before redistributing modern editorial text beyond a clearly defensible exception.
Keep medieval content, provider encoding, repository serialization, and edition rights separate.

open_questions.question: What additional source identifications, variants, dates, and attribution qualifications appear in Reimer's complete introduction, notes, and apparatus?
next_steps: Purchase or consult an authorized copy of the PIMS volume.
Audit each sermon and poem against Reimer's full notes before strengthening item-level claims.
Keep the appendix other-hand sermon outside the digital-selection boundary.

open_questions.question: Can a complete practical-resolution surrogate of the current physical state of Add MS 46919 be obtained and licensed for corpus research?
next_steps: Submit a BL Digitisation Services quotation request for the complete current physical object.
Ask for colour TIFFs covering boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf surface, edges, and detached matter where offered.
Ask BL to enumerate omitted or unphotographable surfaces and provide exact file-to-folio mappings.
Record delivery and redistribution terms separately from underlying medieval-text status.
notes: No order was placed during this research task.

open_questions.question: How should current scholarship resolve the authorship of Poems 7, 22, and 23 and distinguish Herebert's English adaptations from Nicholas Bozon's source responsibility?
next_steps: Consult Reimer's complete poem notes, Gneuss 1960, and newer item-level scholarship.
Preserve the BL exception for item vii and DIMEV's competing attribution rather than choosing silently.
Do not infer Poem 23 authorship solely from codex placement.

open_questions.question: Who performed the March 1996 U-M transformation, when were the small textual interventions introduced, and what specification governed the conversion?
next_steps: Seek U-M or OTA processing documentation.
Complete a character-level diff that separates structural movement from substantive readings.
Preserve both complete payloads and avoid describing the U-M file as a byte copy.

open_questions.question: Do item-level repertories or current scholarship establish collateral witnesses for any selected sermon, outline, or poem beyond Add MS 46919?
next_steps: Search sermon repertories by incipit and biblical theme.
Check modern hymn and Middle English verse studies for lost, parallel, or derivative witnesses.
Do not treat other manuscripts annotated by Herebert as witnesses without textual evidence.
